title: Why all the hate
wikitext:
[]: Why all the hate?             Quote Post by Dan The Cat » Mon Jan 16, 2012 2:26 pm Hello, this is my first topic that i have made for Evageeks but i thought i would throw myself into the deep end. This topic is about is about the rise of hate towards Eva that i have noticed in the past years, i was wondering if other on here have noticed this too. It seems now that it’s the ‘cool’ think to dislike Eva amongst Anime Fan’s, IMO, Even though people haven’t seen they would still it. So, i guessing this might have been done before. but, what do you guys think? (P.s. not good at OP’s )

[]:             Quote Post by Xacebans » Mon Jan 16, 2012 2:36 pm This isn't really anything new. Eva is just polarizing. A lot of people actually dislike it. I also sometimes get the impression that people claim to dislike it despite never having actually seen it--they hear about it from people who have seen it and disliked it and decide, "Omg, must dislike it because apparently ALL THE CHARACTERSES ARE MEANIESSSSS". I think this "rise" in Eva haters is because the internet is so available to people; they can rant about it as much as they want and the haters can form their own little We Love Moeblobs And We Hate Eva communities. Also, despite just how much we fans love it, there's some budget aspects of it that are off-putting for a lot of people. Is somebody who watches robot shows for the action going to want to watch Eva for the plot/characters (its best feature, of course) and turn a blind eye to some of the...er, quality animation? Sure, Eva had some of the best fight animation of its time, and some of it, especially in EoE can stand up to modern animation without a problem. But now to a lot of new anime fans it just looks like generic 90s animation, which can be distasteful when they're used to seeing more modern designs like Code Geass's and such. In short, I think a lot of it is perspective. I don't think Eva will ever really lose its appeal where it counts the most, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Mon Jan 16, 2012 3:04 pm It comes with the nature of something as controversial as Evangelion, but actually, I think it's insanly popular and thoroughly accepted. There was somewhat of a hype backlash in the past, however these days with Rebuild and anime becoming mainstream, it has cemented its popularity even more.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Jan 16, 2012 3:06 pm Short Answer: Hipsters Long answer: It varies really. Eva is most enjoyable if you can relate to the characters, specifically one of the three pilots. A lot of people get turned off quickly because they think Shinji is a whiny bitch, and they either stop before it's explained why he's so screwed up, or they outright don't understand it even after seeing it. The other thing about Eva is, to the uninformed, they may go in expecting something entirely different. They see that it has mechs and monsters so they expect a typical mecha show, when in reality the monsters and mechs are almost secondary to the psychology plot. And then you have the hipsters who hate on anything that's popular. Often times, like you say, they haven't even seen Eva. They just know that it gets tons of praise and is considered good, so being the edgy kids that they are they hate on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 16, 2012 3:47 pm Surprisingly we don't have much in the way of generic hater threads as prior art. There are plenty of "Why is [character] hated?", but nothing else of any substance.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Jan 16, 2012 3:57 pm It's trendy to hate on popular stuff, especially on the internet.

[]:             Quote Post by RangerD11 » Mon Jan 16, 2012 4:13 pm In the words of people in my college class who watch anime and mock Eva on a daily basis - "It's stupid and doesn't make any sense. All Shinji does is whine like an emo and then it turns out everyone is his Mum. Also Asuka is just a bitch. The last 2 episodes are terrible and the show should only be watched so people know what not to do when writing a story." Then they go watch Digimon and Bleach. ........Just a few more months with these people.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Jan 16, 2012 5:08 pm RangerD11 wrote: In the words of people in my college class who watch anime and mock Eva on a daily basis - "It's stupid and doesn't make any sense. All Shinji does is whine like an emo and then it turns out everyone is his Mum. Also Asuka is just a bitch. The last 2 episodes are terrible and the show should only be watched so people know what not to do when writing a story." Then they go watch Digimon and Bleach. ........Just a few more months with these people.

[]:             Quote Post by Dan The Cat » Mon Jan 16, 2012 5:17 pm RangerD11 wrote: In the words of people in my college class who watch anime and mock Eva on a daily basis - "It's stupid and doesn't make any sense. All Shinji does is whine like an emo and then it turns out everyone is his Mum. Also Asuka is just a bitch. The last 2 episodes are terrible and the show should only be watched so people know what not to do when writing a story." Then they go watch Digimon and Bleach. ........Just a few more months with these people.

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Mon Jan 16, 2012 5:18 pm I think like most replies in this thread have stated, sometimes it's just the generic "It's popular, therefore it must be overrated and I will hate it without even watching it." reason for hate of Evangelion. On the other hand, I've seen people who hate Eva simply because of the fact that it was made in 1995. You know, the kind of people who can't watch non-digital animation. There is also the generic "Shinji just whines, Rei is just a puppet, and Asuka's a total bitch." thing.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Mon Jan 16, 2012 5:19 pm CJD wrote: There's a message there about the quality of our high schools.

[]:             Quote Post by Xana » Mon Jan 16, 2012 5:48 pm Some excuses I've heard from people whom I've tried to introduce into Eva: "The animation is old and looks like crap! *goes back to watching Code Geass* " "Omg, there's naked women in the OP = PORN " "It's the work of the devil, if you watch it you'll commit suicide and go to hell" <=== lamest excuse ever "I only watch anime for the pretty drawings" I think the problem with these people is that they're narrow-minded and not willing to try anything different. Also, people who hate on popular stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Mon Jan 16, 2012 6:25 pm One girl I knew watched a few episodes of it (the first ones) and said it wasn't really her thing. I think she watched the wrong half.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Jan 16, 2012 7:28 pm Xana wrote: "Omg, there's naked women in the OP = PORN "

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jan 16, 2012 7:47 pm Xana wrote: "Omg, there's naked women in the OP = PORN "

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Mon Jan 16, 2012 7:49 pm Xana wrote: "Omg, there's naked women in the OP = PORN

[]: Re: Why all the hate?             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Jan 16, 2012 8:19 pm Dan The Cat wrote: This topic is about is about the rise of hate towards Eva that i have noticed in the past years, i was wondering if other on here have noticed this too.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Jan 16, 2012 9:52 pm Aiko Heiwa wrote: Wait there are? Wait, I thought they were just silhouettes.

[]: Re: Why all the hate?             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Mon Jan 16, 2012 10:55 pm symbv wrote: I did not know until somebody mentioned it here in this forum, but then I am not living in the west, nor do I visit English-speaking anime forums. I can guarantee you that there is no hate or stigma associated with Eva in Japan or around the Far East area in Asia.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Mon Jan 16, 2012 11:27 pm Xana wrote: "Omg, there's naked women in the OP = PORN " "I only watch anime for the pretty drawings"
